title: Determination of Density Functional Tight Binding Models for Cerium Allotropes
authors: Nir Goldman, Artem Samtsevych, Chiara Panosetti
abstract: We have developed Density Functional Tight Binding (DFTB) models for cerium that accurately predict both the electronic band structure and energetic ordering of different allotropes. We show that global optimization of the electronic confining potentials minimize the errors in the predicted Kohn-Sham energies while facilitating determination of a many-body repulsive energy. Our results illustrate the ability of DFTB to accurately reproduce complex f-electron interactions for multiple phases while leveraging minimal Density Functional Theory data.
summary: The researchers created a highly efficient machine-learning-enhanced tight-binding model to study cerium, a material with complex f-electron interactions. The model accurately reproduces the electronic and vibrational properties of various cerium phases at a fraction of the computational cost of traditional DFT. This allows for much larger-scale simulations of lanthanide-based materials.
main_problem: Developing a computationally efficient method to accurately simulate the complex f-electron physics and phase stability of cerium allotropes, which are too expensive for standard hybrid-functional DFT.
main_result: The developed DFTB/ChIMES model achieves a 100x increase in computational efficiency over DFT while accurately predicting band structures, phase energetic ordering, and vibrational density of states for various cerium polymorphs.
method: A hybrid approach combining Density Functional Tight Binding (DFTB) with the ChIMES machine-learning formalism to determine the many-body repulsive energy term.
model_or_system: The system consists of cerium (Ce) allotropes (alpha, beta, and delta phases). The model uses a DFTB Hamiltonian with Woods-Saxon type electronic confining potentials and a ChIMES-based many-body expansion (2B, 3B, and 4B) for the repulsive energy.
key_observables: Electronic band structure (topology and dispersion), orbital projections (s, p, d, f), phase stability (energetic ordering), nearest neighbor distances, and vibrational density of states (VDOS).
important_parameters: Hubbard U values (optimized at 0.36 au), Woods-Saxon confinement parameters (r0, a, W), and ChIMES polynomial orders (up to 12).
main_assumptions: ChIMES coefficients are assumed to be permutationally invariant; stress tensor components were omitted from the ChIMES optimization in this specific work.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares DFT and DFTB-PBE band structures for fcc and bcc Ce; Figure 2 shows orbital projections for alpha-Ce; Figures 3 and 4 present DFTB-PBE0 band structures and orbital projections.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of f-electron simulation, describes the development of the DFTB/ChIMES model and its optimization strategy, presents validation against DFT for electronic and structural properties, and discusses the efficiency and transferability of the model.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: DFTB-PBE band structure predictions for fcc (left) and bcc cerium (right). Blue lines correspond to results from DFT and red to DFTB+. Yellow squares in the fcc result correspond to energies used in the DSKO decoding mask.

Figure 2: Band structure projections for α-Ce for s, p, d, and f-orbitals, starting from top to bottom from our DFTB-PBE model. Closed circles correspond to results from DFT and open squares to DFTB+. Red indicates larger orbital projection values, blue is smaller.

Figure 3: DFTB-PBE0 band structure predictions for fcc (left) and bcc cerium (right). Again, blue lines correspond to results from DFT, red to DFTB+, and yellow squares to the DSKO decoding mask. Higher energy conduction bands are removed for the sake of clarity.

Figure 4: Band structure projections for α-Ce for s, p, d, and f-orbitals, starting from top to bottom from our DFTB-PBE0 model. Closed circles correspond to results from DFT and open squares to DFTB+. Red indicates increased orbital projection values.

Figure 5: Vibrational density of states computed from MD simulations with DFTB- PBE at 600 K. The blue line corresponds to results from DFT, the red line to 3-body DFTB/ChIMES, black to 4-body DFTB/ChIMES, and the green to 2-body DFTB/ChIMES.
